folio.label: 65v
folio.l: A moder he hatú , but fader hatú he noon
That I of woot , but shortly in a stounde
He told Alla , how that child was founde
¢ But god woot , q¶ this Senatour also
So vertuous a lyuere , in al my lyf
Ne saw I neuäe , as she , ne herd of moo
Of worldly wo~men , mayden ne of wyf
I dar wel seyn , hire had leuere a knyf
Thurgú out hire breest , than be a wo~man wykke
There is noman , coude bryngö hire to that prykke
¢ Now was this child , as lyke vn to Custaunce
As possible is , a creature for to be
This Alla hatú the face , in remembraunce
Of Dame Custaunce , and ther oon mused he
If that the childes moder , were ougút she
That is his wyf , and preuyly he sigút
And sped hi~ fro the table , that he mygút
¢ îfey thougút he , fantomö is in myn heede
I ougút deme , of skylful Iugement
That in the salt See , my wyf is dede
And afterward he mad , his argument
What woot I , if that Crist haue hidder sent
My wyf by See , as wel as he hire lent
To my contree , from thens that she went
¢ And after noon , hom witú the Senatour
Gooth Alla , for to seen this wonder chaunce
This Senatour dotú Alla , greet honour
And hastily he sent , after Custaunce
But trostetú wel , hire list nat to daunce
Whan æ she wyst , wherfore , was that sond
Vn ethe vp oon hire feet , she mygút stond
¢ Whan Alla saugú his wyf , faire he hire grette
And wepte , æat it was , reuthe for to se
For at the first looke , he on hire sette
He knew wel weraily , that it was she
And she for sorwe , as dombe stant , as tree
So was hire hert shette , in hirhir distresse
Whan she remembred , his vnkyndenesse
¢ Twyes she swougúned , in his owen sigút
He wepetú , and hi~ excusetú pitously
Now god q¶ he , and his halwes brigút
So wysly oon my soule , as haue mäcy
That of ©oure harm , as giltlees am I
As is Maurice my Sone , like ©oure face
Elles the fend , me fecche out of this place
